jokes.text: Is there anything more pathetic than a cowering, emasculated Jew who has to run home to his wife?
jokes.setup: Eddie rushes home to his wife after golf
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal characterization of married Jewish men
jokes.explanation: Sharp, character-specific observation that's both self-deprecating and brutal. Classic Larry perspective.

jokes.text: Imagine how he'd be if he was married to Susie. He'd have pee stains on his pants constantly.
jokes.setup: Discussion of emasculated husbands
jokes.punchline: Vivid image of ultimate marital subjugation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the emasculation theme with specific visual comedy

jokes.text: He has no balls. I have a solid single ball.
jokes.setup: Criticism of Eddie's lack of courage
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly specific personal revelation about his testicle
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Larry moment - turns metaphor literal with uncomfortable personal detail

jokes.text: Yeah, they do not like the Jews. Look at that one.
jokes.setup: Noticing anti-Israel posters in Palestinian restaurant
jokes.punchline: Casual observation while eating their food
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obliviousness to social situations while making uncomfortable observations

jokes.text: What these people should do is send their chicken over to Israel. For the peace process. They'd take down all those settlements in the morning.
jokes.setup: Amazement at the quality of Palestinian chicken
jokes.punchline: Chicken as diplomatic solution to Middle East conflict
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on conflict resolution through food - very Larry to reduce complex geopolitics to chicken quality

jokes.text: This would be a fantastic place for Jews who are cheating on their spouses to come to. Because no Jews ever come here. They'd be so safe.
jokes.setup: Noticing they're the only Jews in the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Perfect hiding spot for Jewish adultery
jokes.explanation: Clever setup that becomes ironically prophetic - great example of Larry's twisted logic

jokes.text: Could be the next Mrs. David. If by some chance she's gonna get over her anti-semitism, odds are not with you, no.
jokes.setup: Larry attracted to Palestinian woman
jokes.punchline: Jeff's assessment of Larry's romantic prospects with someone who hates Jews
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's self-destructive romantic patterns

jokes.text: You're always attracted to someone who doesn't want you, right? Well, here you have somebody who not only doesn't want you, doesn't even acknowledge your right to exist... Wants your destruction. That's a turn-on.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's attraction to unavailable women
jokes.punchline: Escalating to existential rejection as aphrodisiac
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect psychological insight into Larry's character - turns political conflict into dating psychology

jokes.text: I challenged myself to just use mirrors and it didn't work.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining how he hit Ron's car
jokes.punchline: Treating parking like an athletic challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - turning mundane task into unnecessary personal challenge

jokes.text: What do you think this is, the firecracker 400? It's a driveway.
jokes.setup: Larry's mirror-only parking challenge
jokes.punchline: Jeff's NASCAR comparison
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response highlighting the absurdity of Larry's behavior

jokes.text: I went through a bit of a midlife crisis and I rededicated my life to judaism. I had a lot of schpilkas in my life and I didn't realize it.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser wearing yarmulke
jokes.punchline: Using Yiddish to explain his spiritual awakening
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment with Yiddish usage for comedic effect

jokes.text: I'm living under a mitzvah. You have none of that.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Funkhouser's golf absence
jokes.punchline: Religious superiority over golf practice
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor showing Funkhouser's newfound religious pretension

jokes.text: It's a dinner party for adults. Hey, dinner's up.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning why Sammi is at the dinner party
jokes.punchline: His blunt assessment followed by immediate topic change
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry blunt observation with typical awkward transition

jokes.text: Oh, you can't? Really? Watch this. Mmm.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser insisting on wine prayer
jokes.punchline: Larry defiantly drinking without blessing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry rebellious moment - simple but effective character beat

jokes.text: L.O.L., Jeff. L.O.L.
jokes.setup: Someone makes a joke
jokes.punchline: Ilene saying 'L.O.L.' instead of laughing
jokes.explanation: Establishes the brilliant running gag that drives the episode's plot

jokes.text: How in the world, can they dare open up a palestinian chicken restaurant next to the sacred land of that deli?
jokes.setup: Discussion of restaurant location
jokes.punchline: Treating deli as holy ground
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Susie's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: Wow, Larry. They're not-- they're not cold. Do you want me to warm them up for you?
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about cold potatoes
jokes.punchline: Hostess's awkward accommodation attempt
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry social awkwardness moment

jokes.text: A woman goes to a gynecologist. He says, 'get up on the table, put your feet in the stirrups and spread your legs wide.' He goes, 'my God, Mrs. Johnson, you've got a big vagina.'
jokes.setup: Larry starting to tell a joke at dinner
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate gynecology joke
jokes.explanation: Intentionally inappropriate and hack joke that serves the plot but isn't genuinely funny

jokes.text: What's the matter with you? Telling a joke like that in front of her. She's an adult. She can come to an adult dinner party. If she's an adult, why can't she hear a dirty joke?
jokes.setup: Jeff objecting to dirty joke in front of daughter
jokes.punchline: Larry's circular logic about adult status
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic moment highlighting his social obliviousness

jokes.text: She's an adult when she turns 13 or learns to make her first latkes. L.O.L.
jokes.setup: Discussion of when someone becomes an adult
jokes.punchline: Jewish coming-of-age joke followed by L.O.L.
jokes.explanation: Continues the L.O.L. gag while making mild cultural joke

jokes.text: You told me specifically not to let you have any dessert. I appreciate it, Larry, but I changed my mind. Yes yes yes, but you said 'no matter what,' okay? This is the what.
jokes.setup: Juliet wants dessert after asking Larry to stop her
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal interpretation of 'no matter what'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry moment showing his literal-mindedness and commitment to arbitrary rules

jokes.text: Give me the cake! What what what? Larry. Let her have it! It's none of your goddamn business!
jokes.setup: Larry physically preventing Juliet from eating cake
jokes.punchline: Cake tug-of-war erupting at dinner party
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation showing how Larry's principles create chaos

jokes.text: You just speak your mind, you know? You tell people how you're feeling, what you're thinking-- with the potatoes, for example. How cold were those potatoes, by the way? They were freezing.
jokes.setup: Ron praising Larry's honesty
jokes.punchline: Continuing to discuss the potato complaint
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Larry's character trait

jokes.text: She's like-- she's like Susie's twin sister.
jokes.setup: Ron describing his wife's toughness
jokes.punchline: Comparison to the notoriously difficult Susie
jokes.explanation: Good character callback that efficiently establishes Ilene's personality

jokes.text: A date is an experience you have with another person that makes you appreciate being alone. L.O.L. L.O.L.
jokes.setup: Ilene asking Larry about dating
jokes.punchline: Cynical definition of dating followed by double L.O.L.
jokes.explanation: Great Larry observation with perfect cynical worldview, enhanced by L.O.L. gag

jokes.text: Did my pissant husband tell you to do this? No. Yes, he did. No, he did not. Yes, he did.
jokes.setup: Ilene realizing Ron sent Larry to criticize her L.O.L.
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire denial/accusation loop
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation pattern but serves the plot well

jokes.text: You know, in most countries, you could get somebody killed for $573.
jokes.setup: Larry's social assassination mission for car payment
jokes.punchline: Comparison to actual assassination pricing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy line with perfect economy and unexpected perspective

jokes.text: You're a social assassin. So you bungled the hit. What do you know about social assassination? Nothing! I'm the one who named you a social assassin!
jokes.setup: Larry's failed L.O.L. mission
jokes.punchline: Jeff's proprietorial attitude toward the social assassin concept
jokes.explanation: Great callback with Jeff's absurd professional pride in his metaphor

jokes.text: You know, I thought all last night, if rabin can break bread with Arafat, I can have chicken at this anti-semitic shithole.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser arriving at Palestinian restaurant
jokes.punchline: Comparing his dining choice to Middle East peace process
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with historical reference and perfect tone

jokes.text: You're shoving it in their faces. What is this, the raid on Entebbe?
jokes.setup: Funkhouser wanting to wear yarmulke in Palestinian restaurant
jokes.punchline: Larry's comparison to famous Israeli military operation
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd escalation comparing restaurant visit to military raid

jokes.text: Don't you ever touch that. Don't ever grab my yarmulke, ever. How dare you?! Don't ever touch my yarmulke.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to remove Funkhouser's yarmulke
jokes.punchline: Funkhouser's intense protective reaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing religious awakening vs. practical concerns

jokes.text: A big Jew. Big. A big Jew and you still told him to take off his Jew cap. Thank you, my friend.
jokes.setup: Palestinian woman observing Larry's conflict with Funkhouser
jokes.punchline: Appreciation for Larry's secular stance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment setting up Larry-Shara dynamic

jokes.text: Eddie and Ilene? He's cheating on Juliet? Holy shit. Oh my God!
jokes.setup: Larry spotting Eddie and Ilene together
jokes.punchline: Larry's shock and realization of the implications
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction comedy to surprising revelation

jokes.text: Eddie and Ilene? He's cheating on Juliet? Holy shit. All right, listen. Juliet is great friends with Susie. If he's cheating on Juliet, I can't be here to see it. I am out of here.
jokes.setup: Larry spotting Eddie with Ilene
jokes.punchline: Larry's panic about social complications
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to earlier joke with Larry's social anxiety

jokes.text: Out of all the hummus joints in all the world, you had to walk into mine.
jokes.setup: Awkward encounter at Palestinian restaurant
jokes.punchline: Casablanca reference with Middle Eastern twist
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that fits the situation

jokes.text: The only reason we're here together now is because she had a big fight with Ron, okay? Well, there's always been a lot of tension there, but let's just say this was the straw that broke the camel's back.
jokes.setup: Eddie explaining his affair with Ilene
jokes.punchline: L.O.L. complaint as relationship catalyst
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing unintended consequences of Larry's actions

jokes.text: The only reason we're here together now is because she had a big fight with Ron, okay? So she reached out as a friend. We always liked each other and now we like each other a little more. Maybe a lot more.
jokes.setup: Eddie explaining his relationship with Ilene
jokes.punchline: Escalating admission from friendship to romance
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic confession with mild escalation

jokes.text: I guess I shouldn't say that here.
jokes.setup: Eddie using 'camel's back' idiom in Middle Eastern restaurant
jokes.punchline: Realizing the cultural inappropriateness
jokes.explanation: Clever meta moment about cultural sensitivity in language

jokes.text: He doesn't like the way she keeps saying 'L.O.L.' It's a wonderful thing to say. I love when she says 'L.O.L.' That's one of the reasons we're together.
jokes.setup: Eddie explaining what caused the marital rift
jokes.punchline: Eddie finds attractive what drove Ron crazy
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal showing how same trait can repel or attract

jokes.text: I think if you didn't want to sleep with her, you wouldn't be so tolerant of that 'L.O.L.'
jokes.setup: Eddie's tolerance for Ilene's L.O.L. habit
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical but accurate assessment of sexual motivation
jokes.explanation: Sharp psychological insight delivered with Larry's typical bluntness

jokes.text: In other words, no matter what? Huh? No matter what.
jokes.setup: Eddie promising not to tell about the affair
jokes.punchline: Larry invoking the episode's recurring phrase with pointed emphasis
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reinforces the episode's central theme with dramatic irony

jokes.text: So then he tells me the only reason that they're having this affair was because of the 'L.O.L.' Thing. I drove her to it. I was the straw that broke the camel's back.
jokes.setup: Larry recounting Eddie's explanation
jokes.punchline: Larry accepting responsibility for the affair
jokes.explanation: Excellent convergence of multiple plot threads with Larry's guilt

jokes.text: I was the straw that broke the camel's back. All marriages have straws. Right, the camel's carrying a lot of straw. That's what a marriage is.
jokes.setup: Larry feeling responsible for Eddie and Ilene's affair
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor about marriage as straw-carrying camel
jokes.explanation: Good extended metaphor about marriage dynamics with absurd logic

jokes.text: You look like Blofeld. I look like I've got a kitty and we're making evil plans.
jokes.setup: Larry with decorative pillow on his lap
jokes.punchline: James Bond villain comparison
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy reference that fits the character moment

jokes.text: every time after my mom takes a sip of something, she makes this really obnoxious sound. She smacks her lips together and does this little 'ahh' thing.
jokes.setup: Sammi wanting to talk to Larry
jokes.punchline: Her detailed description of her mother's annoying drinking habit
jokes.explanation: Good setup for another social assassination job with specific behavioral detail

jokes.text: Look, I'm flattered, kid, that you would consider me for such a delicate assignment, but I have to tell you I'm-- I'm out of that business. I'm not your man.
jokes.setup: Sammi asking Larry to confront her mother about lip-smacking
jokes.punchline: Larry treating social assassination like organized crime retirement
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with Larry's professional assassin persona

jokes.text: You would do that? Try me.
jokes.setup: Sammi threatening to reveal the affair
jokes.punchline: Teenage blackmail confidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the daughter's manipulative skills

jokes.text: You would do that? Try me.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing he's being blackmailed by a teenager
jokes.punchline: Sammi's cold challenge
jokes.explanation: Great power dynamic reversal with economical menacing delivery

jokes.text: Boy, you really are your mother's daughter, aren't you? Yeah, now get the fuck out of my driveway, you bald prick.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing Sammi's manipulative nature
jokes.punchline: Teenager's shocking vulgarity and aggression
jokes.explanation: Shocking character reversal with perfectly inappropriate teenage language

jokes.text: Fuck me, you fucking Jew! Filthy Jew. You zionist pig. You occupying fuck. I'm going to fuck the Jew out of you.
jokes.setup: Larry and Shara's sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Anti-Semitic dirty talk as foreplay
jokes.explanation: Absolutely shocking and original comedy concept - turning hatred into sexual role-play

jokes.text: Fuck me, you fucking Jew! Filthy Jew. You zionist pig. You occupying fuck. Occupy this. I'm going to fuck the Jew out of you.
jokes.setup: Larry and Shara in intimate situation
jokes.punchline: Racist dirty talk as foreplay
jokes.explanation: Shocking subversive comedy mixing racism with sexuality - pushing boundaries effectively

jokes.text: This reminds me of something Theodor Herzl once said.
jokes.setup: Intense sexual encounter with political role-play
jokes.punchline: Academic Zionist reference during sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Larry moment - intellectualizing the most inappropriate moment

jokes.text: Fuck me like Israel fucks my people! Show me the promised land, huh? Labe, son of Nat. Keep my father out of it, will you?
jokes.setup: Continued racist dirty talk
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical objection to involving his father in the roleplay
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Larry's practical boundaries during absurd sexual roleplay

jokes.text: Labe, son of Nat. Keep my father out of it, will you?
jokes.setup: Shara using Larry's full name during sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's protective reaction about his father
jokes.explanation: Great callback with Larry maintaining dignity about his father during bizarre encounter

jokes.text: 'We seek him here, we seek him there, those frenchies seek him everywhere, is he in heaven, is he in hell? That damned elusive pimpernel.'
jokes.setup: Post-coital moment
jokes.punchline: Reciting Scarlet Pimpernel poetry
jokes.explanation: Bizarre Larry intellectual moment in inappropriate context

jokes.text: 'Fuck me, Jew bastard'? Yeah, small price to pay for the best sex I've ever had anywhere.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Larry about the anti-Semitic dirty talk
jokes.punchline: Larry's cost-benefit analysis of hatred-based sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy line showing Larry's twisted priorities - quotable and shocking

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. The penis doesn't care about race, creed and color. The penis wants to get to his homeland. It wants to go home.
jokes.setup: Discussion of attraction despite political differences
jokes.punchline: Penis as Zionist immigrant metaphor
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Larry philosophy - turning sexual desire into immigration metaphor, incredibly quotable

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. The penis doesn't care about race, creed and color. The penis wants to get to his homeland. It wants to go home.
jokes.setup: Discussing attraction despite racial tensions
jokes.punchline: Larry's philosophical statement about penis nationalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist philosophy mixing sexual and political metaphors - quotable wisdom

jokes.text: You're koufaxing us? Well, if you're saying that your best player is unable to play because it's the sabbath, yes, I'm koufaxing you.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser can't play golf on Saturday due to religious observance
jokes.punchline: Sandy Koufax reference as verb for religious sports abstinence
jokes.explanation: Excellent cultural reference turned into verb - shows deep knowledge of Jewish sports history

jokes.text: Why don't you go wrap your head in a towel? You're not even a man anymore! Mommy rabbi says don't play, little boy!
jokes.setup: Argument with Funkhouser about religious observance
jokes.punchline: Larry's cruel mockery of religious commitment
jokes.explanation: Effective character moment showing Larry's cruelty when frustrated

jokes.text: Well, keep that away from me no matter what. Give it to me! You said, 'no matter what'! Give me that! Give me that!
jokes.setup: Rabbi seeing Palestinian chicken and wanting it despite earlier request
jokes.punchline: Exact repeat of Juliet's cake scenario with religious authority
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows the universal nature of temptation, even for religious leaders

jokes.text: Every time you take a sip... You make this noise-- 'ahh.' I'm enjoying it. Eh. It's kinda annoying.
jokes.setup: Larry interrupting golf to confront Susie about her lip-smacking
jokes.punchline: Terrible timing for social assassination
jokes.explanation: Great callback with perfect terrible timing showing Larry's compulsiveness

jokes.text: I told Larry to tell you. 'Cause I told him if he didn't tell you then I'd say something about Eddie and Ilene having an affair. What?! Eddie and Ilene are having an affair?!
jokes.setup: Sammi confessing to manipulation
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing the affair while explaining the blackmail
jokes.explanation: Perfect domino effect moment where all the plot threads collapse simultaneously

jokes.text: L.O.L., honey! L.O.L.! Fuck you!
jokes.setup: Susie discovering her husband's affair
jokes.punchline: Ironically using L.O.L. in moment of rage
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of L.O.L. gag - using it ironically in genuine anger, brilliant writing

jokes.text: Larry! Come! Join us over here! No, Larry. Come to this side and I'm yours. Larry, I have a sister. You, me and Jasmine-- the three of us.
jokes.setup: Larry caught between competing protests
jokes.punchline: Both sides desperately recruiting Larry with increasingly absurd offers
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity as both sides compete for Larry's allegiance with sexual bribes

jokes.text: Larry, I have a sister. You, me and Jasmine-- the three of us. All right, that's enough! Just get the fuck over here! Just get the fuck over here. Stop being an idiot.
jokes.setup: Both sides competing for Larry's loyalty
jokes.punchline: Escalating from political appeals to sexual bribes to angry demands
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing desperation on both sides with absurd bribes
